title: The Christian.
content:
Honour and happiness unite
    To make the Christian�s name a praise;
    How fair the scene, how clear the light,
    That fills the remnant of his days!


    A kingly character he bears,
    No change his priestly office knows;
    Unfading is the crown he wears,
    His joys can never reach a close.


    Adorn�d with glory from on high,
    Salvation shines upon his face;
    His robe is of the ethereal dye,
    His steps are dignity and grace.


    Inferior honours he disdains,
    Nor stoops to take applause from earth:
    The King of kings himself maintains
    The expenses of his heavenly birth.


    The noblest creature seen below,
    Ordain�d to fill a throne above;
    God gives him all he can bestow,
    His kingdom of eternal love.


    My soul is ravish�d at the thought!
    Methinks from earth I see him rise!
    Angels congratulate his lot,
    And shout him welcome to the skies!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Cowper delivers a powerful performance in "The Christian."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
